title: Changelog
text: Real-time retrieval therapeutic, diagnostic, prognostic evidence OncoKB MAF annotator + web API somatic variants - requires valid API token (see new CLI argument --oncokb_api_token) Evidence retrieved SNV/InDels (via HGVSp protein change HGVSg genomic change), gene fusions, copy number alterations Tumor type context passed OncoKB via OncoTree codes, enabling tumor-specific evidence retrieval exact -tumor-type matches OncoKB annotation includes mutation effect (e.g. Gain--function), oncogenicity (e.g. Oncogenic, Likely Oncogenic), clinical evidence items (treatment sensitivity/resistance, diagnostic prognostic implications) --oncokb_api_token — valid OncoKB API token required enable feature --oncokb_oncotree_code — override tumor-type OncoTree code used OncoKB queries (default: derived --tumor_site); useful desired OncoTree code specific site-level mapping --oncokb_exclusive — restrict biomarker reporting OncoKB , skipping CIViC CGI sources; intended workflows single curated source preferred absolute (default): thresholds applied total copy number directly relative: thresholds expressed fold-change tumor ploidy combined: absolute relative thresholds must satisfied Amplification: --cna_amp_threshold_absolute (default: 5), --cna_amp_threshold_relative (default: 2.5×) Gain: --cna_gain_threshold_absolute (default: 3), --cna_gain_threshold_relative (default: 1.5×) Heterozygous deletion: --cna_del_threshold_absolute (default: 1), --cna_del_threshold_relative (default: 0.5×) Allele-specific copy numbers (major + minor) used correct handling loss heterozygosity (LOH) sex-chromosome dosage tumor ploidy provided user, PCGR estimates allele-specific copy number distribution fallback (used relative combined modes) Two-hit candidate annotation: segments overlapping known tumor suppressor gene flagged somatic loss--function SNV/InDel CNA deletion event present (requiring CPSR input germline hit detection) New CLI argument --input_rna_fusion accepts TSV file RNA fusion calls (e.g. STAR-Fusion, Arriba, similar callers) Fusions displayed dedicated section HTML report, annotated gene-level oncogenicity actionability OncoKB API token provided, fusions queried OncoKB API clinical evidence (therapeutic, diagnostic, prognostic) mutation effect interactive variant biomarker evidence tables now use reactable place previous DT/DataTables implementation Tables support column-level filtering, global search, compact nested detail rows (expandable per-variant evidence panels) — providing responsive feature-rich browsing experience hidden SEARCH_INDEX column concatenates key fields (gene, alteration, consequence, oncogenicity) enable full-text search across clinically relevant columns

title: Changelog
text: Loss--function annotation MaxEntScan now stratum-aware: canonical splice site positions (±2 bp exon–intron boundary) always set LOSS_OF_FUNCTION = TRUE; non-canonical positions, variants Strong MaxEntScan evidence tier (donor positions +3 +6; acceptor positions -3 -20) additionally set LOSS_OF_FUNCTION = TRUE — Moderate Supporting tiers positions informational Tumor-germline filtering now uses unified per-variant gnomAD allele frequency flag (gnomAD_AF_ABOVE_TOLERATED) considers available population frequencies exome genome gnomAD datasets, preferring exome values available

title: Changelog
text: multiple options related RMarkdown output now deprecated Users need download assembly-specific VEP cache separately Ensembl VEP website, provide path new required argument --vep_dir pcgr command Re-engineered data bundle generation pipeline HTML report overview contents data bundle shipped reference data , also available (grch38). Singularity/Apptainer support Moved code base initial Python workflow steps (biomarker matching, CNA segment annotation, RNA expression analysis, oncogenicity classification) Variants now classified respect oncogenicity actionability, previous global tier classification (tier 1-5) thus deprecated New argument n_copy_gain - Minimum number total copy number segments considered gains/amplifications (default: 6) --input_rna_expression - accepts TSV file gene expression values --expression_sim - boolean flag enable expression similarity analysis --expression_sim_db - Comma-separated string databases used RNA expression similarity analysis, default: tcga,depmap,treehouse --tmb_display - Type TMB measure show report (coding_and_silent, coding_non_silent, missense_only) --tmb_dp_min - Minimum depth position considered TMB calculation (default: 0) - requires allelic support information VCF --tmb_af_min - Minimum allele frequency position considered TMB calculation (default: 0) - requires allelic support information VCF multi-sheet Excel workbook output analysis output provided, suitable e.g. aggregation results across samples --pcgr_dir renamed --refdata_dir --clinvar_ignore_noncancer renamed --clinvar_report_noncancer, meaning variants found ClinVar, yet attributed non-cancer related phenotypes, now excluded reporting default --vep_gencode_all renamed --vep_gencode_basic, meaning gene variant annotation now using GENCODE transcripts default, basic set --preserved_info_tags renamed --retained_info_tags --basic renamed --no_reporting --target_size_mb renamed --effective_target_size_mb LOFTEE plugin VEP removed loss--function variant classifier (due low level maintenance, outdated dependency requirements). now, simplified LoF-annotation used replacement, looking primarily CSQ types (stop_gained, frameshift_variant, splice_acceptor_variant, splice_donor_variant). Furthermore, frameshift/stop-gain variants found within last 5% coding sequence length deemed non-LOF, splice donor variants disrupting canonical site (GC>GT). even advanced LoF-annotation planned future release. Biomarkers matched much comprehensively previous versions, matching genomic level, codon, exon, amino acid gene level (principal non-principal transcript matches)

title: Input files
text: PCGR workflow accepts following main input files: unannotated, single-sample VCF file (>= v4.2) called somatic variants (SNVs/InDels) file allele-specific copy number segments (tab-separated values - TSV) file RNA fusion transcripts detected tumor (tab-separated values - TSV) file transcript/gene expression levels (tab-separated values - TSV) input VCF required input file, copy number, RNA fusion, gene expression files optional. following arguments pcgr command used input files: --input_vcf (required argument pcgr) --input_cna --input_rna_fusion --input_rna_expression addition main input files, user can also opt provide panel--normals VCF file (--pon_vcf) tumor-variant filtering, well input file CPSR-classified germline variants (TSV) (--input_cpsr).

title: Installation
text: PCGR requires following data run successfully: Reference bundle - containing data multiple knowledge resources, including information molecular biomarkers, targeted cancer therapies, variant frequencies etc. Key datasets include CIViC, CGI, Open Targets Platform, TCGA, ClinVar. Ensembl VEP data cache - needed variant annotation VEP (Variant Effect Predictor) User-supplied sample-specific inputs - e.g. somatic variant calls VCF format PCGR supports GRCh37 GRCh38 human genome assemblies. data need match chosen assembly.

title: Installation
text: Reference bundles generated semi-automatically (PCGR author) versioned based release date. Keep mind bundles support certain Ensembl VEP versions, importantly also specific software versions PCGR. Upgrading PCGR software without upgrading bundle specified thus recommended installation strategy. latest (v20260508) genome-specific bundles can downloaded directly (size: ~5G): Tip 1: data/grch3x/.PCGR_BUNDLE_VERSION file within downloaded bundle indicates bundle version reporting purposes. Tip 2: data/grch3x/data_overview.grch3x.html file provides report overview statistics key resources included reference bundle.

title: Installation
code: BUNDLE_VERSION="20260508" GENOME="grch38" # or "grch37" BUNDLE="pcgr_ref_data.${BUNDLE_VERSION}.${GENOME}.tgz" wget https://insilico.hpc.uio.no/pcgr/${BUNDLE} gzip -dc ${BUNDLE} | tar xvf -  mkdir ${BUNDLE_VERSION} mv data/ ${BUNDLE_VERSION}

title: Installation

title: Installation
text: Conda support Linux machines. following process can take anywhere 10 40 minutes installing scratch, mostly depending user’s server’s internet connection. time spent downloading {BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19} {BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38} R packages (happens end conda environment creation).
code: PCGR_VERSION="2.2.5.9015" # set up variables PCGR_REPO="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sigven/pcgr/v${PCGR_VERSION}/conda/env/lock/" PLATFORM="linux" # create conda envs in local directory mkdir pcgr_conda conda create --prefix ./pcgr_conda/pcgr --file ${PCGR_REPO}/pcgr-${PLATFORM}-64.lock conda create --prefix ./pcgr_conda/pcgrr --file ${PCGR_REPO}/pcgrr-${PLATFORM}-64.lock # you need to specify the directory of the conda env when using --prefix conda activate ./pcgr_conda/pcgr # test that it works pcgr --version pcgr --help

title: Installation
text: PCGR Docker image available GitHub Container Registry https://github.com/sigven/pcgr/pkgs/container/pcgr. Pull latest v2.2.5.9015 image :

title: Installation
text: example , assume following directory structure: /Users//dir1 - directory working /Users//dir1/VEP_cache - directory downloaded VEP cache (Note: directory contain homo_sapiens directory child directory /Users//dir1/20260508 - directory downloaded PCGR reference bundle (Note: directory contain directory named data child directory) /Users//dir1/pcgr_inputs - directory input files (e.g. VCF files) /Users//dir1/pcgr_outputs - directory want store PCGR outputs NOTE: need run Docker-based version PCGR non-root user, may need explicitly add options quarto work properly, .e. --env "XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/quarto_cache_home" (Singularity/Apptainer , see also issue #246).
code: PCGR_VERSION="2.2.5.9015" BUNDLE_VERSION="20260508" docker container run -it --rm \     -v /Users/you/dir1/VEP_cache:/mnt/.vep \     -v /Users/you/dir1/${BUNDLE_VERSION}:/mnt/bundle \     -v /Users/you/dir1/pcgr_inputs:/mnt/pcgr_inputs \     -v /Users/you/dir1/pcgr_outputs:/mnt/pcgr_outputs \     ghcr.io/sigven/pcgr:${PCGR_VERSION} \     pcgr \       --input_vcf "/mnt/pcgr_inputs/T001-BRCA.grch38.vcf.gz" \       --vep_dir "/mnt/.vep" \       --refdata_dir "/mnt/bundle" \       --output_dir "/mnt/pcgr_outputs" \       --genome_assembly "grch38" \       --sample_id "SAMPLE_B" \       --tumor_dp_tag "TDP" \       --tumor_af_tag "TAF" \       --assay "WGS" \       --vcf2maf

title: Installation
text: PCGR Singularity/Apptainer image available GitHub Container Registry. Pull latest v2.2.5.9015 image : download Singularity Image File (SIF) called pcgr_2.2.5.9015.singularity.sif can run Singularity Apptainer.

title: Installation
text: Note: Apptainer/Singularity issues directly related PCGR, recommend reaching Apptainer community (e.g. https://github.com/apptainer/apptainer) since limited experience Apptainer/Singularity.
code: PCGR_VERSION="2.2.5.9015" BUNDLE_VERSION="20260508" apptainer exec \   --writable-tmpfs \   --no-home \   --env "XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/quarto_cache_home" \   -B /Users/you/dir1/VEP_cache:/mnt/.vep \   -B /Users/you/dir1/${BUNDLE_VERSION}:/mnt/bundle \   -B /Users/you/dir1/pcgr_inputs:/mnt/pcgr_inputs \   -B /Users/you/dir1/pcgr_outputs:/mnt/pcgr_outputs \   pcgr_${PCGR_VERSION}.singularity.sif \   pcgr \     --input_vcf "/mnt/pcgr_inputs/T001-BRCA.grch38.vcf.gz" \     --vep_dir "/mnt/.vep" \     --refdata_dir "/mnt/bundle" \     --output_dir "/mnt/pcgr_outputs" \     --genome_assembly "grch38" \     --sample_id "SAMPLE_B" \     --assay "WGS" \     --tumor_dp_tag "TDP" \     --tumor_af_tag "TAF" \     --vcf2maf

what: Somatic copy number variant class thresholds
title: Running
text: copy number segments provided (--input_cna), PCGR classifies segment amplification, gain, heterozygous deletion, homozygous deletion based configurable thresholds. thresholding strategy controlled : --cna_threshold_mode <absolute|relative|combined> (default: absolute) Three modes supported: absolute — thresholds expressed total copy numbers (integer values). segment classified based total copy number (nMajor + nMinor), irrespective tumor ploidy. relative — thresholds expressed fold-change estimated tumor ploidy. mode requires --tumor_ploidy provided; , PCGR fall back absolute mode. combined — segment must satisfy absolute relative threshold criteria assigned given class. conservative mode. per-class thresholds can configured : Segments total copy number zero always classified homozygous deletions, regardless threshold mode. additional option controls genes considered affected CNA event: --cna_transcript_overlap_pct <value> — minimum mean percent overlap copy number segment gene transcripts required report gene affected (default: 50)

--input_rna_fusion INPUT_RNA_FUSION                         File with RNA fusion transcripts detected in tumor (tab-separated values)   --input_rna_expression INPUT_RNA_EXP                         File with bulk RNA expression counts (TPM) of transcripts in tumor (tab-separated values)  SNV/InDel analysis options:   --vep_dir VEP_DIR     Directory of VEP cache, e.g. $HOME/.vep (required when EFFECTIVE_TARGET_SIZE_MB                         Effective target size in Mb (potentially limited by read depth) of sequencing                         assay (for TMB analysis) (default: 34 (WES/WGS)) Ignore non-coding (i.e. non protein-altering) variants in report, default: False   --vep_n_forks VEP_N_FORKS                         Number of forks (VEP option '--fork'), default: 4 title: Variant classification
text: PCGR prioritizes evaluates variants according clinical actionability. Currently, PCGR implements tier classification framework along proposed AMP/ASCO/CAP guidelines, outlined Li et al., 2017: reported within tumor type/class tumor question (query tumor sample), strong clinical evidence (.e. FDA-approved/standard--care therapies, part guidelines, validated discovered late clinical trials (OncoKB evidence levels 1/2/3A, CIViC evidence levels /B)) strong clinical evidence tumor types/classes one specified user (.e. (OncoKB evidence level 3B), weak clinical evidence (early trials, case reports etc. (CIViC evidence levels C/D/E), OncoKB evidence level 4) tumor type/class specified user coding variants, observed significant allele frequencies (gnomAD MAF < 0.001), found oncogenes tumor suppressor genes, yet linked known predictive, prognostic, diagnostic biomarkers CIViC database Cancer Biomarkers Database PCGR, skip classification variants AMP/ASCO/CAP-specified Tier IV (benign/likely benign variants), rather take cautious approach. Specifically, SNVs/indels fall tier , II, III, classify Tier V: coding variants, includes protein-coding variants non-cancer related genes, well Tier VI: non-coding variants, includes synonymous variants, intronic variants, variants non-coding regions.

title: Personal Cancer Genome ReporteR
text: Personal Cancer Genome Reporter (PCGR) stand-alone software package functional annotation translation individual tumor genomes precision cancer medicine. interprets primarily somatic SNVs/InDels copy number aberrations, additional support interpretation bulk RNA-seq expression data. software classifies variants respect predicted oncogenicity, clinical actionability. Interactive HTML output reports allow user interrogate clinical impact molecular findings individual tumor. according oncogenicity: evaluating oncogenic potential somatic DNA aberrations (ClinGen/CGC/VICC guidelines) according actionability: mapping therapeutic, diagnostic, prognostic implications somatic DNA aberrations (AMP/ASCO/CAP guidelines) Tumor mutational burden (TMB) estimation Mutational signature analysis Microsatellite instability (MSI) classification RNA-seq support - gene expression outlier detection, sample similarity analysis, immune contexture profiling PCGR supports recent human genome assemblies (GRCh37/GRCh38), accepts variant calls tumor-control tumor-sequencing assays. Much functionality intended whole-exome/whole-genome sequencing assays, can also apply PCGR output targeted sequencing panels. interested interrogation germline variants relation cancer predisposition, recommend trying accompanying tool Cancer Predisposition Sequencing Reporter (CPSR). Example screenshots quarto-based cancer genome report PCGR:  PCGR originates Norwegian Cancer Genomics Consortium (NCGC), Institute Cancer Research, Oslo University Hospital, Norway.
